title: SMUHSD Work Permits
summary: SMUHSD says under-18 students needing a new or renewed work permit should download, print, and complete the B1-1 Request for Work Permit; student, parent/guardian, and employer sections are required.

title: California Department of Education - Work Permits for Students
summary: CDE states only work permits issued by a California school district are valid in California; entertainment work permits use a separate process.

title: California DIR - Child Labor Laws
summary: California DIR maintains child labor law guidance covering work hours, age requirements, restrictions, employer requirements, and work permits.

title: California DIR - 2026 Minimum Wage
summary: California DIR states the statewide minimum wage effective January 1, 2026 is $16.90 per hour.

title: San Mateo County Jobs for Youth
summary: County of San Mateo Jobs for Youth serves youth ages 14-21 and offers job-readiness support.

title: Jobs for Youth Job Board
summary: Jobs for Youth maintains a youth-focused job board with employment, internship, and volunteer opportunities in San Mateo County.

title: Burlingame High School Work Permits
summary: BHS notes students must reapply and obtain a new work permit at the beginning of each school year and allow 48-72 hours for processing.

title: Aragon High School Work Permits
summary: AHS work permit instructions require physical signatures from employer and parent/guardian and student completion of the minor/school section.

title: San Mateo High School Work Permits
summary: SMHS lists work permit requirements including good attendance, minimum 2.0 GPA, and parent/guardian permission.
